title: NOC 41220 – Secondary School Teachers | GTR Immigration
description: Secondary School Teachers under NOC 41220 are licensed education professionals responsible for teaching academic, vocational, and specialized subjects to students at the high school level across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role is central to Canada’s public and private education systems. These educators prepare students for post-secondary education, skilled trades, and entry into the workforce, while supporting their academic, social, and personal development.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group412 – Professional occupations in education servicesMinor Group4122 – Secondary, elementary and kindergarten school teachersNOC Code41220

common_job_titles: English as a Second Language (ESL) high school teacher
French as a Second Language teacher – secondary school
duties: Secondary School Teachers perform some or all of the following duties:
Prepare and deliver subject material according to approved curriculum
Teach through lectures, discussions, audio-visual presentations, and lab/shop sessions
Evaluate student progress and determine learning needs
Communicate progress with parents and school administrators
Participate in staff meetings and professional development workshops
Teach online or distance education courses
Advise students on academic and career pathways
employment_requirements: Often preceded by a Bachelor’s degree in Arts or Sciences (for academic subjects)
Vocational teachers require specialized training or trade experience
In Quebec, trade instructors require apprenticeship completion and trade certification
Several years of teaching experience for department head roles
Additional training for:Special educationESL or FSL (French as a Second Language)
ESL or FSL (French as a Second Language)
Membership in provincial teachers’ association may be required
Foreign-trained teachers must obtain credential assessment and provincial certification before practicing.
salary_prospects: Secondary School Teachers typically earn between:
$38,500 – $111,400 per year, depending on province, years of experience, and subject specialization.
Teachers in Northern territories often receive additional allowances and benefits.
job_outlook: The employment outlook for Secondary School Teachers is stable and positive due to:
Increasing need for bilingual and special education teachers
Competition can vary by province and subject area.
labour_market_demand: Secondary School Teachers are employed in:
Expansion of special education and ESL programs
Growth in vocational and trade education streams
Opportunities are available nationwide, particularly in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, and Northern territories.
High demand in rural and northern regions
Clear pathway to department head and school leadership roles
Teachers specializing in STEM subjects, French immersion, ESL, and special education are especially in demand.
pathways: Secondary School Teachers qualify under high-skilled TEER 1 immigration categories and may apply through:
Foreign educators must complete provincial certification after immigration.
